mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of May 2008 reflects Fed rate cuts from prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points within 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in May 2008, near cyclical lows before housing crisis acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and future consumer wealth destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1425k units in April 2008, down sharply from 2006 peak of 2.3M units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite housing starts collapse, or correlation between HOUST changes and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.52 in May 2008, showing deceleration from prior year growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth declines while unemployment rises simultaneously, indicating consumer demand destruction and reduced earnings visibility. This joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premium expands.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth < 2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp within 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 689.4B in May 2008, UNRATE at 3.7%, both showing early stress signals
regime_hint: demand shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS × UNRATE) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns following joint trigger are not significantly lower than single-series triggers (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 50k monthly additions while inflation remains elevated, signaling stagflationary pressure and policy uncertainty. This combination produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 50k AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153.5M in May 2008, CPIAUCSL at 298.35 reflecting elevated inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns following joint trigger do not differ significantly from single-series triggers (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling economic stagnation that reduces corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20039.4B in Q1 2008, showing deceleration from prior year
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of instances following sub-1.5% GDP growth, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains above 3.5% while inflation-adjusted real rates turn positive, signaling monetary tightness that constrains equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices discount rates upward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% with elevated inflation creates positive real rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Real rate interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day returns following positive real rate signals are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
